prompt: Explain the water cycle, in prose, without using any lists, bullets, headings, or numbered steps. Keep it to one paragraph.
response: Water drifts skyward when the sun warms oceans, lakes, and moist soil, sending vapor adrift on rising currents that cool and swell into silvery clouds; carried by winds, these clouds roam until they meet chilled air that cradles droplets into heavier forms that tumble as rain, snow, or quiet drizzle, sliding over rooftops, streaming along hillsides, and seeping softly into earth where roots drink and springs emerge to feed creeks that wander ever downward, loosening grains of rock and soil, merging with rivers that pulse toward lowlands, surrendering once more to the open mouth of the sea, where the same sun bears down again, coaxing the same water upward in a quiet eternal circuit that moistens every leaf, every lung, every life between cloud and wave.
